title: Off-Grid Circuit Analysis & Electronics
subject: Electrical Engineering & Applied Physics
description: Master Ohm's Law, DC circuit wiring, solar charge controllers, battery banks, and multimeter diagnostics.
learningObjectives: Apply Ohm's Law (V = I * R) and Power equations (P = V * I).
Calculate series vs parallel battery bank voltage and amp-hour capacity.
Troubleshoot DC circuits using a digital multimeter.

lessons.title: Ohm's Law & Power Calculations
lessons.content: Voltage (V) is electrical pressure, Current (I) is electron flow rate in Amperes, and Resistance (R) opposes flow in Ohms. Ohm's Law dictates V = I * R. Power in Watts equals Voltage times Current (P = V * I). A 100W LED running on 12V DC consumes I = 100 / 12 = 8.33 Amperes.

lessons.title: Series vs Parallel Battery Configuration
lessons.content: Wiring two 12V 100Ah batteries in SERIES doubles voltage to 24V while keeping capacity at 100Ah. Wiring them in PARALLEL keeps voltage at 12V while doubling capacity to 200Ah.

flashcards.definition: Mathematical relationship between Voltage, Current, and Resistance (V = I * R).
Circuit configuration where components are connected end-to-end, adding voltages.
Circuit configuration where components share common nodes, adding current capacity.

questions.question: What is the total voltage of two 12-Volt batteries wired in SERIES?
If a 12V circuit draws 5 Amps of current, what is the power output in Watts?
Which meter setting is used to test continuity and verify an unbroken wire connection?

practicalChallenge.title: Multimeter Circuit & Battery Voltage Audit
practicalChallenge.instruction: Use a digital multimeter to measure open-circuit DC voltage on a 12V battery or AA cell. Calculate power draw for a small light bulb!
